title: Press escape to exit timeline
excerpt: After I set a date in the timeline column, I have to click somewhere outside of the calendar box to exit.  It would be easy if you could simply press escape for it to set the currently selected date range.  Or, alternati…

posts.author: swoltornist (Steven Woltornist)
posts.cookedHtml: After I set a date in the timeline column, I have to click somewhere outside of the calendar box to exit. It would be easy if you could simply press escape for it to set the currently selected date range. Or, alternatively, make an OK button. Thanks!

posts.author: TaylorTedesco (Taylor Tedesco)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes agreed! Would love to be able to use the ESC key for lots of things.

posts.author: timlittletech (Tim Little)
posts.cookedHtml:
I agree there seems to be a bug that after selecting timeline the escape shortcut doesn’t work.

if you look at mondays shortcut list it should work: after a quick test if you just open the dialog and don’t set a date then esc successfully closes the box.

I think there is a bug because if you click anywhere in the dialog after selecting your date timeline and then press esc the dialog closes.

This would be a great useability win to have ESC shortcut work after setting the timeline.
